video.title: What were Movistar THINKING?! | Volta a Catalunya Stage 1 2021

transcript.clean_text: welcome back to the lantern rouge youtube channel and the highlights of stage one of the hundredth edition the volta cyclist catalunya the loop starting and finishing as it normally does on the coast in kalea and then going inland for some climbs 178k stage and these climbs weren't particularly steep and shouldn't be too threatening to a sprinter in good condition but with this sort of profile we knew a break could win like the kent in 2019 as well as some sprinters being called out on the climb and there being a reduced bunch sprint now this year's edition of catalunya we've got chris froome nairo man peter sagan after a good result of melania sanorama despite not looking himself at toreno adriatico we didn't really know how he'd go richard carapaz leading ineos with adam yates as well as mark here she's first race of the season andrew almeida most are of course bringing the goat trident of valverde mars and solaire if you want more background on the stages and the start list in much more detail i've got the big preview video i put up a couple of days link down below predictably a break went away early there's the kom jersey on offer for the rider that could mop up the most points in the middle of this stage from the break the fall that made it being monica tarame martin and barhani rui kosher had a bad crash and had to abandon i think peter serry was caught up in that crash as well before live images started where we saw borah hansgrove pacing the break back now we didn't know whether it was for mayous or segan we presumed sagan who was the favorite for this stage they had benz vihoff pacing on the front and they also used either shelling to pace now they might not have had too much of a choice kelderman and cameron are writing for their own gc and they're presumably hoping the stage would be easy so mass and sagan could get over the hills and contest the sprint but shelling packs a lot of punch and these climbs were never going to be a problem for him but he had to work a lot this stage into a mother's start on that second to last longer climb on the steepest section started to pace really hard dropping peter sagan with over five kilometers left in the climb motherstar used the six percent steeper section of the climb at the start to put riders like sagan maeus under pressure max canter on dsm a quick man as well being dropped monaco was the last remnant of the breakaway that hadn't been mopped up by motherstar on the climb he was going for the kom points at the end of this climb but mother's day were reading into his gap so so quickly chris froome started yo-yoing but monica managed to hang on by about 20 seconds to mother star charging behind him to take the kom jersey and pretty much had to stop pedaling he did a full gas effort there pedrero verona cemetery i think were pacing hard and that put chris froome into difficulty he made it over the main climb but then there was a false descent and then they climbed up a little bit more and he got popped just at the end before they began the 10 kilometer plus technical descent and motherstar didn't let up there either they paced really hard on the descent they were even causing mini splits mark solaire actually unintentionally lost the wheel to valverde and two other runners in front of him on the descent but he actually caught back up to them i thought perhaps mother star were going to try something like on the welter stage 2 in 2020 where they paced really hard then got on the front on the descent and then unleashed mark solaire who actually took their biggest win of the season in stage two of welter last year but they didn't and they burnt i think pedrero until they got into the short valley before the last climb and they sat up they have carls verona still here and i think they're joking with ineos asking iniesta pace but there's no incentive for ineos or jumbo visma the gc team to pace right now there's no break up the road that they need to keep in check there's nothing for their gc riders to gain in the flat run into the finish so ineos had no interest in pacing quick men still in this group with dion smith on bike exchange and camp for trek sega fredo but with no one controlling anything and mother star not pacing attacks started to fly thick and fast maznada a couple of times lotto sudall throwing three or four riders at every move including crone the dane as well as great thomas for ineos getting involved in an early move following maznada trek also had elisande brambila and chicone trying to get into a few of these moves late but a move didn't stick until lewis leon sanchez spanish national champ attacked gapping jonas finger guard with crone on his will and there was no reaction from behind a lot of saddam blocking half the road except for leonard campner i thought writing for gc with remy rochas on his wheel the confidence rider trying to bridge across to this lewis leon sanchez move and no one paced for several kilometers motherstar didn't pace ineos and jumbo visna weren't too concerned about kemner going up the road for gc they didn't want to give him more than 40 45 seconds and you can see some kilometers later with about 10 k's to go either shelling's moving up to block bike exchange had like five or six riders in this group that they were badly positioned at the back callum scotson in front of dion smith on the right hand side here you'd think with dion smith being the quickest man in this group and peter sagan having been dropped the bike exchange would have paced early to control that move they didn't they waited until the last climb for esteban chavez to eventually start pacing but it was too late at that point the gap was out to 35 seconds chavos was pretty much left on his own to try and bring back a group of four who were working well together until leonard camner attacked them and it was too late for trek running for camp in the sprint marxilla had a puncture in the last three k's i think he got given the same time as everybody on gc but someone from the group of four was going to win the stage jumbo viz mourinhos as i said just keeping that gap in camera in check not letting it blow out to anything crazy he seemed to be going for the stage primarily anyway he wasn't just pacing them in full gas trying to maintain his gap on gc a few hundred meters to go crohn puts himself fourth wheel behind roshas and louisiana sanchez he can mark both of them rosh has launches really early at 250 meters to go and kind of pinches louis leon sanchez between kemna and himself which allows crone to get an unencumbered run at the line to the left-hand side beating louis lino sanchez easily his first world tour race and he wins the stage a great day for lotto cidale and about 16 seconds behind it was dion smith on the left hand side as we look at it of alexander cam for trek segafredo taking out the bunch sprint for fifth ahead of maharich and shelling and despite it being his first world tour race you've got to say crone really pulled a fast one on louis leon sanchez who maybe didn't expect him to be so quick much better positioning from him than louis leon sanchez and he got that magic draft off rochas to launch him past everybody but the big question i want to ask is what was mother star's ultimate plan today pacing the drops again on the main climb totally get it makes perfect sense they've got valverde in the group they can drop some of the other sprinters like mouse as well and can to put them under pressure not attacking on the descent it was pretty far from the finish that makes sense too not committing to fully pacing in the valley as well when they've only got verona left i get that too they still had dion smith in this group who at this point in valverde's career is way quicker than valverde in a bunch sprint so maybe they wanted a bit of chaos they wanted to split up again and get riders into a smaller group rather than the 60 plus man group they had with about 25 k's to go but what makes zero sense is when louis leon sanchez rostana goes up the road and they still have riders in the group behind they've still got valverde for a reduced bunch sprint they've still got solaire that they could be getting in a move and they don't pace with verona or solaire or try and bridge across at all they just plonk they'll vote a second wheel who pretty much blocks and disrupts the chase to the forward head here's the results andres crone for a lot of saddam winning the stage ahead of lewis leon sanchez roshas and kamna 16 seconds ahead of smith maurice shelling still came seventh after doing all that work valverde camp and valgrind krone going into the leaders jersey but i think he'll lose that today the 18 and a half kilometer time trial and stage two 200 meters elevation gain in this tt around banolos a lot of the riders will be familiar with this area just outside of girona favor for the stage remy cavania after that parinis fantastic itt but rowan dennis could probably win this too this is the big opportunity for almeida and mcnulty to put as much time as possible into the stronger climbers of the gc contenders ahead of the mountainous stages in three and four i hope you enjoyed the video like it down below if you did we've obviously got immediate reactions to each catalonia stage on the lantern rouge cycling podcast uploaded soon after the stage finishes but i'll see you with the highlights for stage two tomorrow ciao bye
